user.title: WashData Setup
user.description:
Configure your washing machine or other appliance.

Power sensor is required.

**Next, you will be asked if you want to create your first profile.**

data_description.name: A friendly name for this device (e.g., 'Washing Machine', 'Dishwasher').
data_description.device_type: What type of appliance is this? Helps tailor detection and labeling.
data_description.power_sensor: The sensor entity that reports real-time power consumption (in watts) from your smart plug.
data_description.min_power: Power readings above this threshold (in watts) indicate the appliance is running. Start with 2W for most devices.

first_profile.title: Create First Profile
first_profile.description:
A **Cycle** is a complete run of your appliance (e.g., a load of laundry). A **Profile** groups these cycles by type (e.g., 'Cotton', 'Quick Wash'). Creating a profile now helps the integration estimate duration immediately.

You can manually select this profile in the device controls if it's not detected automatically.

**Leave 'Profile Name' empty to skip this step.**

apply_suggestions.title: Copy Suggested Values

apply_suggestions_confirm.title: Review Suggested Changes
apply_suggestions_confirm.description:
WashData found **{pending_count}** suggested value(s) to apply.

Changes:
{changes}

✅ Check the box below and click **Submit** to apply and save these changes immediately.
❌ Leave it unchecked and click **Submit** to cancel and go back.

settings.title: Settings
settings.description:
{deprecation_warning}Tune detection thresholds, learning, and advanced behavior. Defaults work well for most setups.

Suggested Settings currently available: {suggestions_count}.
If this is above 0, open Advanced Settings and use Apply Suggested Values to review recommendations.

data.notify_people: Delay Delivery Until These People Are Home
data.notify_only_when_home: Delay Notifications Until Selected Person Is Home

data_description.device_type: Select the type of appliance (Washing Machine, Dryer, Dishwasher, Coffee Machine, EV, Bread Maker, Pump). This tag is stored with each cycle for better organization.
data_description.power_sensor: The sensor entity reporting real-time power (in watts). You can change this anytime without losing historical data — useful if you replace a smart plug.
data_description.min_power: Power readings above this threshold (in watts) indicate the appliance is running. Start with 2W for most devices.
data_description.off_delay: Seconds the smoothed power must stay below the stop threshold to mark completion. Default: 120s.
data_description.notify_actions: Optional Home Assistant action sequence to run for notifications (scripts, notify, TTS, etc.). If set, actions run before per-event service targets.
data_description.notify_people: People entities used for presence gating. When enabled, notification delivery is delayed until at least one selected person is home.
data_description.notify_only_when_home: If enabled, delay notifications until at least one selected person is home.
data_description.notify_fire_events: If enabled, fire Home Assistant events for cycle start and end (ha_washdata_cycle_started and ha_washdata_cycle_ended) to drive automations.
data_description.notify_start_services: One or more notify services to alert when a cycle begins. Leave empty to skip start notifications.
data_description.notify_finish_services: One or more notify services to alert when a cycle finishes or nears completion. Leave empty to skip finish notifications.
data_description.notify_live_services: One or more notify services to receive live progress updates while the cycle runs (mobile companion app only). Leave empty to skip live updates.
data_description.notify_before_end_minutes: Minutes before the estimated end of the cycle to trigger a notification. Set to 0 to disable. Default: 0.
data_description.notify_title: Custom title for notifications. Supports {device} placeholder.
data_description.notify_icon: Icon to use for notifications (e.g. mdi:washing-machine). Leave empty to send no icon.
data_description.notify_start_message: Message sent when a cycle starts. Supports {device} placeholder.
data_description.notify_finish_message: Message sent when a cycle finishes. Supports {device}, {duration}, {program}, {energy_kwh}, {cost} placeholders.
data_description.notify_pre_complete_message: Text of the recurring live progress updates while the cycle runs. Supports {device}, {minutes}, {program} placeholders.

notifications.title: Notifications
notifications.description: Configure notification targets per event type and optional live mobile progress updates. Each event type can be sent to a different set of recipients.

data.notify_people: Delay Delivery Until These People Are Home
data.notify_only_when_home: Delay Notifications Until Selected Person Is Home

data_description.go_back: Tick this and click Submit to discard any changes above and return to the main menu.
data_description.notify_actions: Optional Home Assistant action sequence to run for every notification event (scripts, notify, TTS, etc.). Actions fire before the per-event service targets.
data_description.notify_people: People entities used for presence gating. When enabled, notification delivery is delayed until at least one selected person is home.
data_description.notify_only_when_home: If enabled, delay notifications until at least one selected person is home.
data_description.notify_fire_events: If enabled, fire Home Assistant events for cycle start and end (ha_washdata_cycle_started and ha_washdata_cycle_ended) to drive automations.
data_description.notify_start_services: One or more notify services to alert when a cycle begins (e.g. notify.mobile_app_my_phone). Leave empty to skip start notifications.
data_description.notify_finish_services: One or more notify services to alert when a cycle finishes or nears completion. Leave empty to skip finish notifications.
data_description.notify_live_services: One or more notify services to receive live progress updates while the cycle runs (mobile companion app only). Leave empty to skip live updates.
data_description.notify_before_end_minutes: Minutes before the estimated end of the cycle to trigger a notification. Set to 0 to disable. Default: 0.
data_description.notify_live_interval_seconds: How often progress updates are sent while running. Lower values are more responsive but consume more notifications. A minimum of 30 seconds is enforced - values below 30 s are automatically rounded up to 30 s.
data_description.notify_live_overrun_percent: Safety margin above the estimated update count for long/overrunning cycles (for example 20% allows 1.2x estimated updates).
data_description.notify_live_chronometer: When enabled, each live update includes a chronometer countdown to the estimated finish time. The notification timer ticks down automatically on the device between updates (Android companion app only).
data_description.notify_title: Custom title for notifications. Supports {device} placeholder.
data_description.notify_icon: Icon to use for notifications (e.g. mdi:washing-machine). Leave empty to send no icon.
data_description.notify_start_message: Message sent when a cycle starts. Supports {device} placeholder.
data_description.notify_finish_message: Message sent when a cycle finishes. Supports {device}, {duration}, {program}, {energy_kwh}, {cost} placeholders.
data_description.notify_pre_complete_message: Text of the recurring live progress updates while the cycle runs. Supports {device}, {minutes}, {program} placeholders.
data_description.notify_reminder_message: Text of the one-time reminder sent the configured number of minutes before the estimated end. Distinct from the recurring live updates above. Supports {device}, {minutes}, {program} placeholders.
data_description.notify_timeout_seconds: Automatically dismiss notifications after this many seconds (forwarded as the companion app 'timeout'). Set to 0 to keep them until dismissed. Default: 0.
data_description.notify_channel: Android companion app notification channel for status, live progress, and reminder notifications. The sound and importance of a channel are configured in the companion app the first time the channel name is used - WashData only sets the channel name. Leave empty for the app default.
data_description.notify_finish_channel: Separate Android channel for the finished alert (also used by the reminder and the laundry-waiting nag) so it can have its own sound. Leave empty to reuse the channel above.
data_description.energy_price_entity: Select a numeric entity that holds the current electricity price (e.g. sensor.electricity_price, input_number.energy_rate). When set, enables the {cost} placeholder. Takes precedence over the static value if both are configured.
data_description.energy_price_static: Fixed electricity price per kWh. When set, enables the {cost} placeholder. Ignored if an entity is configured above. Add your currency symbol in the message template, e.g. {cost} €.

advanced_settings.title: Advanced Settings
advanced_settings.description: Tune detection thresholds, learning, and advanced behavior. Defaults work well for most setups.

suggestions_section.name: Suggested Settings
suggestions_section.description: {suggestions_count} learned suggestions available. Tick Apply Suggested Values to review proposed changes before saving.

detection_section.name: Detection & Power Thresholds
detection_section.description: When the appliance is considered ON or OFF, energy gates, and timing for state transitions.

data_description.start_duration_threshold: Ignore brief power spikes shorter than this duration (seconds). Filters out boot spikes (e.g., 60W for 2s) before real cycle starts. Default: 5s.
data_description.start_energy_threshold: Cycle must accumulate at least this much energy (Wh) during the START phase to be confirmed. Default: 0.2 Wh.
data_description.completion_min_seconds: Cycles shorter than this will be marked as 'interrupted' even if they finish naturally. Default: 600s.
data_description.start_threshold_w: Power must rise ABOVE this threshold to START a cycle. Set higher than your standby power. Default: min_power + 1 W.
data_description.stop_threshold_w: Power must fall BELOW this threshold to END a cycle. Set just above zero to avoid noise triggering false ends. Default: 0.6 * min_power.
data_description.end_energy_threshold: Cycle ends only if energy in the last Off-Delay window is below this value (Wh). Prevents false ends if power fluctuates near zero. Default: 0.05 Wh.
data_description.running_dead_zone: After cycle starts, ignore power dips for this many seconds to prevent false end detection during initial spin-up phase. Set to 0 to disable. Default: 3s.
data_description.end_repeat_count: Number of times the end condition (power below threshold for off_delay) must be met consecutively before ending the cycle. Higher values prevent false ends during pauses. Default: 1.
data_description.min_off_gap: If a cycle starts within this many seconds of the previous one ending, they will be MERGED into a single cycle.
data_description.sampling_interval: Minimum sampling interval in seconds. Updates faster than this rate will be ignored. Default: 30s (2s for washing machines, washer-dryers, and dishwashers).

matching_section.name: Profile Matching & Learning
matching_section.description: How aggressively WashData matches running cycles against learned profiles and when to ask for feedback.

data_description.profile_match_min_duration_ratio: Minimum duration ratio for profile matching. Running cycle must be at least this fraction of profile duration to match. Lower = earlier matching. Default: 0.10 (10%).
data_description.profile_match_interval: How often (in seconds) to attempt profile matching during a cycle. Lower values provide faster detection but use more CPU. Default: 300s (5 minutes).
data_description.profile_match_threshold: Minimum DTW similarity score (0.0–1.0) required to consider a profile as a match. Higher = stricter matching, fewer false positives. Default: 0.4.
data_description.profile_unmatch_threshold: DTW similarity score (0.0–1.0) below which a previously matched profile is rejected. Should be lower than match threshold to prevent flickering. Default: 0.35.
data_description.auto_label_confidence: Automatically label cycles at or above this confidence on completion (0.0–1.0).
data_description.learning_confidence: Minimum confidence to request user verification via an event (0.0–1.0).
data_description.suppress_feedback_notifications: When enabled, WashData will still track and request feedback internally but will not show persistent notifications asking you to confirm cycles. Useful when cycles are always detected correctly and you find the prompts distracting.
data_description.duration_tolerance: Tolerance for time-remaining estimates during a run (0.0–0.5 corresponds to 0–50%).
data_description.smoothing_window: Number of recent power readings used for moving-average smoothing.
data_description.profile_duration_tolerance: Tolerance used by profile matching for total duration variance (0.0–0.5). Default behavior: ±25%.

timing_section.name: Timing, Maintenance & Debug
timing_section.description: Watchdog, progress reset, auto-maintenance, and debug entity exposure.

data_description.watchdog_interval: Seconds between watchdog checks while running. Default: 30s. WARNING: Ensure this is HIGHER than your sensor's update interval to avoid false stops.
data_description.no_update_active_timeout: For publish-on-change sensors: only force-end an ACTIVE cycle if no updates arrive for this many seconds. Low-power completion still uses the Off Delay.
data_description.progress_reset_delay: After a cycle completes (100%), wait this many seconds of idle before resetting progress to 0%. Default: 1800s.
data_description.auto_maintenance: Enable auto-maintenance (repair samples and perform routine cleanup).
data_description.expose_debug_entities: Show advanced sensors (Confidence, Phase, Ambiguity) for debugging.
data_description.save_debug_traces: Store detailed ranking and power trace data in history (Increases storage usage).

anti_wrinkle_section.name: Anti-Wrinkle Shield (Dryers)
anti_wrinkle_section.description: Ignore post-cycle drum rotations to prevent ghost cycles. Dryer / washer-dryer only.

data_description.anti_wrinkle_enabled: Ignore short low-power drum rotations after a cycle ends to prevent ghost cycles (dryer/washer-dryer only).
data_description.anti_wrinkle_max_power: Bursts at or below this power are treated as anti-wrinkle rotations instead of new cycles. Only applies when Anti-Wrinkle Shield is enabled.
data_description.anti_wrinkle_max_duration: Maximum burst length to treat as anti-wrinkle rotation. Only applies when Anti-Wrinkle Shield is enabled.
data_description.anti_wrinkle_exit_power: If power drops below this level, exit anti-wrinkle immediately (true off). Only applies when Anti-Wrinkle Shield is enabled.

delay_start_section.name: Delayed Start Detection
delay_start_section.description: Treat sustained low-power standby as 'Waiting to Start' until the cycle actually begins.

data_description.delay_start_detect_enabled: When enabled, sustained low power between Stop Threshold and Start Threshold (W) is treated as a delayed start. The state sensor shows 'Waiting to Start' until power crosses Start Threshold (W) for long enough to count as a real cycle. Tune Start Threshold (W) above any background tumble/anti-damp power your appliance draws while waiting.
data_description.delay_confirm_seconds: Power must stay between Stop Threshold (W) and Start Threshold (W) for this many seconds before WashData enters 'Waiting to Start'. Filters out brief menu-navigation peaks. Default: 60 s.
data_description.delay_timeout_hours: Safety timeout: if the machine is still in 'Waiting to Start' after this many hours, WashData resets to Off. Default: 8 h.

external_triggers_section.name: External Triggers, Door & Pause
external_triggers_section.description: Optional external end-trigger sensor, door sensor for pause/clean detection, and pause-power-cut switch.

data_description.external_end_trigger_enabled: Enable monitoring of an external binary sensor to trigger cycle completion.
data_description.external_end_trigger: Select a binary sensor entity. When this sensor triggers, the current cycle will end with status 'completed'.
data_description.external_end_trigger_inverted: By default, the trigger fires when the sensor turns ON. Check this box to fire when the sensor turns OFF instead.
data_description.door_sensor_entity: Optional binary sensor for the machine door (on = open, off = closed). When the door opens during an active cycle, WashData will confirm the cycle as intentionally paused. After the cycle ends, if the door is still closed, the state changes to 'Clean' until the door is opened. Note: closing the door does NOT auto-resume a paused cycle - resume must be triggered manually via the Resume Cycle button or service.
data_description.pause_cuts_power: When pausing via the Pause Cycle button or service, also turn off the configured switch entity. Only takes effect if a switch entity is configured for this device.
data_description.switch_entity: The switch entity to toggle when pausing or resuming. Required when 'Cut Power When Pausing' is enabled.
data_description.notify_unload_delay_minutes: Send a notification via the finish notification channel after the cycle ends and the door has not been opened for this many minutes (requires Door Sensor). Set to 0 to disable. Default: 60 min.

device_link_section.name: Device Link
device_link_section.description: Optionally link this WashData device to an existing Home Assistant device (for example the smart plug or the appliance itself). The WashData device is then shown as "Connected via" that device. Leave empty to keep WashData as a standalone device.

data_description.linked_device: Select the device to connect WashData to. This adds a 'Connected via' reference in the device registry; WashData keeps its own device card and entities. Clear the selection to remove the link.

pump_section.name: Pump Monitor
pump_section.description: Pump device type only. Fires an event if a pump cycle runs past this duration.

data_description.pump_stuck_duration: If a pump cycle is still running after this many seconds, a ha_washdata_pump_stuck event is fired once. Use this to detect a jammed motor (typical sump pump run is under 60 s). Default: 1800 s (30 min). Pump device type only.

diagnostics.title: Diagnostics & Maintenance

menu_options.wipe_history: Wipe ALL data for this device (irreversible)

clear_debug_data.title: Clear Debug Data
clear_debug_data.description: Are you sure you want to delete all stored debug traces? This will free up space but remove detailed ranking info from past cycles.

manage_phase_catalog.title: Manage Phase Catalog
manage_phase_catalog.description:
Phase catalog (defaults for this device + custom phases):
{phase_summary}

phase_catalog_create.description: Create a custom phase name and description, and choose which device type it applies to.

phase_catalog_edit_select.description: Select a custom phase to edit.

phase_catalog_delete.title: Delete Custom Phase
phase_catalog_delete.description: Select a custom phase to delete. Any assigned ranges using this phase will be removed.

assign_profile_phases.title: Assign Phase Ranges

assign_profile_phases_add.title: Add Phase Range
assign_profile_phases_add.description: Add one phase range to the current draft.

assign_profile_phases_delete.title: Delete Phase Range
assign_profile_phases_delete.description: Select a range to remove from the draft.

assign_profile_phases_auto_detect_name.description:
Profile: **{profile_name}**

**{detected_count} phase(s) detected:**
{ranges_summary}

Assign a name to each phase.

assign_profile_phases_select.title: Assign Phase Ranges
assign_profile_phases_select.description: Select a profile to configure phase ranges.

create_profile.title: Create New Profile
create_profile.description:
Create a new profile manually or from a past cycle.

Profile name examples: 'Cotton 40°C', 'Eco', 'Quick Wash', 'Cotton + Cupboard Dry'.

**What the matcher uses:** power-consumption shape, total cycle duration, and total energy. It does not read temperature or spin settings directly.

**Profiles that will be reliably auto-detected:** programs with clearly different durations or power patterns - e.g. a 20-minute Quick Wash vs a 3-hour Cotton cycle, or a wash-only cycle vs a wash+dry combo (which typically runs 2-3× longer).

**Profiles that may need manual selection:** variants of the same program that differ only in temperature or spin speed (e.g. Cotton 40°C vs Cotton 60°C) often produce similar power shapes. The system will still attempt to distinguish them and learn from your corrections over time, but expect to confirm matches manually at first.

**Washer-dryer tip:** create separate profiles for wash-only and wash+dry runs - the duration and energy difference is large enough for reliable auto-detection.

data.unlabel_cycles: Remove label from cycles using this profile

select_cycle_to_delete.title: Delete Cycle
select_cycle_to_delete.description: ⚠️ This will permanently delete the selected cycle

post_process.title: Process History (Merge/Split)
post_process.description: Clean up cycle history by merging fragmented runs and splitting incorrectly merged cycles within a time window. Enter number of hours to look back (use 999999 for all).

data_description.time_range: Number of hours to scan for fragmented cycles to merge. Use 999999 to process all historical data.
data_description.gap_seconds: Threshold to decide split vs merge. Gaps SHORTER than this are merged. Gaps LONGER than this are split. Lower this to force a split on a cycle that was merged incorrectly.

cleanup_profile.title: Clean Up History - Select Profile
cleanup_profile.description: Select a profile to visualize. This will generate a graph showing all past cycles for this profile to help identify outliers.

cleanup_select.title: Clean Up History - Graph & Delete
cleanup_select.description:
![Graph]({graph_url})

**Visualizing {profile_name}**

The graph shows individual cycles as colored lines. Identify outliers (lines far from the group) and matching their color in the list below to delete them.

**Select cycles to PERMANENTLY delete:**

interactive_editor.title: Merge/Split Interactive Editor
interactive_editor.description: Select a manual action to perform on your cycle history.

editor_split_params.title: Split Method
editor_split_params.description: Choose how to split this cycle. **Auto-detect** finds idle gaps in the power data; **Manual timestamp** lets you set explicit split points (useful when one cycle ran straight into the next with no idle gap between them).

editor_split_auto_params.title: Auto-Split Configuration
editor_split_auto_params.description: Adjust the sensitivity for splitting this cycle. Any idle gap longer than this will cause a split.

editor_split_manual_params.title: Manual Split Timestamps
editor_split_manual_params.description:
{preview_md}Cycle window: **{cycle_start_wallclock} → {cycle_end_wallclock}** on {cycle_date}.

Enter one or more split timestamps (`HH:MM` or `HH:MM:SS`), one per line. The cycle will be cut at each timestamp — N timestamps produce N+1 segments.

reprocess_history.title: Maintenance: Reprocess & Optimize Data
reprocess_history.description:
Performs deep cleaning of historical data:
1. Trims zero-power readings (silence).
2. Fixes cycle timing/duration.
3. Recalculates all statistical models (envelopes).

**Run this to fix data issues or apply new algorithms.**

wipe_history.title: Wipe History (Testing Only)
wipe_history.description: ⚠️ This will permanently delete ALL stored cycles and profiles for this device. This cannot be undone!

export_import.title: Export/Import JSON
export_import.description: Copy/paste cycles, profiles, AND settings for this device. Export below or paste JSON to import.

data_description.mode: Choose Export to copy current data and settings, or Import to paste exported data from another WashData device.
data_description.json_payload: For Export: copy this JSON (includes cycles, profiles, and all fine-tuned settings). For Import: paste JSON exported from WashData.

record_cycle.title: Record Cycle
record_cycle.description:
Manually record a cycle to create a clean profile without interference.

Status: **{status}**
Duration: {duration}s
Samples: {samples}

record_process.title: Process Recording
record_process.description:
![Graph]({graph_url})

**Graph shows raw recording.** Blue = Keep, Red = Proposed Trim.
*Graph is static and does not update with form changes.*

Recording stopped. Trims are aligned to the detected sampling rate (~{sampling_rate}s).

Raw Duration: {duration}s
Samples: {samples}

learning_feedbacks_pick.title: Pick Feedback to Review
learning_feedbacks_pick.description: Select a cycle review request to open.

learning_feedbacks_dismiss_all.title: Dismiss All Pending Feedbacks
learning_feedbacks_dismiss_all.description:
You are about to dismiss **{count}** pending feedback request(s). Dismissed cycles remain in your history but will not contribute new learning signal. This cannot be undone.

✅ Check the box below and click **Submit** to dismiss all.
❌ Leave it unchecked and click **Submit** to cancel and go back.

data.confirm_dismiss_all: Confirm: dismiss all pending feedback requests

resolve_feedback.title: Resolve Feedback
resolve_feedback.description:
{comparison_img}{candidates_table}
**Detected Profile**: {detected_profile} ({confidence_pct}%)
**Estimated Duration**: {est_duration_min} min
**Actual Duration**: {act_duration_min} min

__Choose an action below:__

data.action: What would you like to do?

trim_cycle_select.title: Trim Cycle - Select Cycle
trim_cycle_select.description: Select a cycle to trim. ✓ = completed, ⚠ = resumed, ✗ = interrupted

trim_cycle_start.title: Set Trim Start

trim_cycle_end.title: Set Trim End

error.import_failed: Import failed. Please paste a valid WashData export JSON.
error.select_exactly_one: Select exactly one cycle for this action.
error.select_at_least_one: Select at least one cycle for this action.
error.select_at_least_two: Select at least two cycles for this action.
error.profile_exists: A profile with this name already exists
error.rename_failed: Failed to rename profile. Profile may not exist or new name is already taken.
error.assignment_failed: Failed to assign profile to cycle
error.duplicate_phase: A phase with this name already exists.
error.invalid_phase_range: Phase range is invalid. End must be greater than start.
error.invalid_phase_timestamp: Timestamp is invalid. Use YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM or HH:MM format.
error.overlapping_phase_ranges: Phase ranges overlap. Adjust the ranges and try again.
error.incomplete_phase_row: Each phase row must include a phase plus complete start/end values for the selected mode.
error.timestamp_mode_cycle_required: Please select a source cycle when using timestamp mode.

error.suggestions_ready_notification_message:
The **Suggested Settings** sensor now reports **{count}** actionable recommendations.

To review and apply them: **Settings > Devices & Services > WashData > Configure > Advanced Settings > Apply Suggested Values**.

Suggestions are optional and shown for review before you save.
error.trim_range_invalid: Start must be before end. Please adjust your trim points.
error.trim_failed: Trim failed. The cycle may no longer exist or the window is empty.
error.invalid_split_timestamp: Timestamp is invalid or outside the cycle window. Use HH:MM or HH:MM:SS within the cycle's window.
error.no_split_segments_found: No valid segments could be produced from these timestamps. Make sure each timestamp is inside the cycle window and segments are at least 1 minute long.
error.auto_tune_suggestion: {device_type} {device_title} detected ghost cycles. Suggested min_power change: {current_min}W -> {new_min}W (not applied automatically).
error.auto_tune_fallback: {device_type} {device_title} detected ghost cycles. Suggested min_power change: {current_min}W -> {new_min}W (not applied automatically).

abort.no_split_segments_found: No splittable segments were found in the selected cycle.
abort.cycle_not_found: The selected cycle could not be loaded.
abort.no_power_data: No power trace data available for the selected cycle.
abort.no_profiles_found: No profiles found. Create a profile first.
abort.no_profiles_for_matching: No profiles available for matching. Create profiles first.
abort.no_suggestions: No suggested values available yet. Run a few cycles and try again.
abort.debug_data_cleared: Successfully cleared debug data from {count} cycles.

options.settings_deprecation_warning: ⚠️ **Deprecated device type:** {device_type} is scheduled for removal in a future release. WashData's matching pipeline does not produce reliable results for this appliance class. Your integration keeps working through the deprecation period; to silence this warning, switch **Device Type** below to one of the supported types (Washing Machine, Dryer, Washer-Dryer Combo, Dishwasher, Air Fryer, Bread Maker, or Pump), or to **Other (Advanced)** if your appliance does not match any of the supported types. **Other (Advanced)** ships intentionally generic defaults that are not tuned for any specific appliance, so you will need to configure thresholds, timeouts, and matching parameters yourself; all your existing settings are preserved when you switch.
options.editor_select_info: Select 1 cycle to split, or 2+ cycles to merge.
options.phase_preview_no_curve: Average profile curve is not available yet. Run/label more cycles for this profile.
options.not_enough_data_graph: Not enough data to generate graph.
options.graph_legend_body: The blue band represents the minimum and maximum power draw range observed. The line shows the average power curve.
options.split_preview_confirm_fmt: Click Confirm to split this cycle into {count} separate cycles.
options.merge_preview_joining_fmt: Joining {count} cycles. Gaps will be filled with 0W readings.

profile_name.name: Profile Name

reference_cycle_id.name: Reference Cycle ID

confidence_threshold.description: Minimum match confidence (0.50-0.95) to apply labels.

export_config.name: Export Config
export_config.description: Export this device's profiles, cycles, and settings to a JSON file (per device).

path.name: Path
path.description: Optional absolute file path to write (defaults to /config/ha_washdata_export_{entry}.json).

import_config.name: Import Config
import_config.description: Import profiles, cycles, and settings for this device from a JSON export file (settings may be overwritten).

device_id.name: Device
device_id.description: The WashData device to import into.

path.name: Path
path.description: Absolute path to the export JSON file to import.

submit_cycle_feedback.name: Submit Cycle Feedback
submit_cycle_feedback.description: Confirm or correct an auto-detected program after a completed cycle. Provide either `entry_id` (advanced) or `device_id` (recommended).

device_id.name: Device
device_id.description: The WashData device (recommended instead of entry_id).

entry_id.name: Entry ID
entry_id.description: The config entry id for the device (alternative to device_id).

cycle_id.name: Cycle ID
cycle_id.description: The cycle id shown in the feedback notification / logs.

user_confirmed.name: Confirm Detected Program
user_confirmed.description: Set true if the detected program is correct.

corrected_profile.name: Corrected Profile
corrected_profile.description: If not confirmed, provide the correct profile/program name.

record_start.name: Record Cycle Start
record_start.description: Start manually recording a clean cycle (bypasses all matching logic).

device_id.name: Device
device_id.description: The WashData device to stop recording.

trim_cycle.name: Trim Cycle
trim_cycle.description: Trim the power data of a past cycle to a specific time window.

cycle_id.name: Cycle ID
cycle_id.description: The ID of the cycle to trim.

trim_start_s.name: Trim Start (seconds)
trim_start_s.description: Keep data from this offset in seconds. Default 0.

trim_end_s.name: Trim End (seconds)
trim_end_s.description: Keep data up to this offset in seconds. Default = full duration.

pause_cycle.name: Pause Cycle
pause_cycle.description: Pause the active cycle for a WashData device.

resume_cycle.name: Resume Cycle
resume_cycle.description: Resume a paused cycle for a WashData device.

no_config_entry.message: No config entry found for device.

integration_not_loaded.message: Integration not loaded for this device.

cycle_not_found_or_no_power.message: Cycle not found or has no power data.

trim_failed_empty_window.message: Trim failed - cycle not found, no power data, or resulting window is empty.

trim_invalid_range.message: trim_end_s must be greater than trim_start_s.
